steps.title: Simplify the first equation to slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Start with $4x - 6y = 10y + 2$. Combine the $y$ terms on the left:
$$4x - 16y = 2$$
Rearrange to slope-intercept form:
$$y = \frac{1}{4}x - \frac{1}{8}$$
The slope of the first line is $\frac{1}{4}$.

steps.title: Rewrite the second equation in slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Start with $ty = \frac{1}{2} + 2x$, or equivalently $ty = 2x + \frac{1}{2}$. Divide both sides by $t$:
$$y = \frac{2}{t}x + \frac{1}{2t}$$
The slope of the second line is $\frac{2}{t}$.

steps.title: Set slopes equal for parallel lines with no solution
steps.content:
For a system of linear equations to have no solution, the lines must be parallel—same slope but different $y$-intercepts. Set the slopes equal:
$$\frac{2}{t} = \frac{1}{4}$$
Cross-multiply:
$$8 = t$$
Verify: when $t = 8$, the second line is $y = \frac{1}{4}x + \frac{1}{16}$, and the first line is $y = \frac{1}{4}x - \frac{1}{8}$. Both have slope $\frac{1}{4}$ but different $y$-intercepts, confirming they are parallel with no intersection. Therefore, $t = 8$.
